semanticCore: Refuge, shelter, hiding place, fortress, escape, or search
quranicRelevance: No refuge from God except in Him (Malja' - 'an la malja'a mina Allahi'), and searching for refuge ('malja'an').

keyDerivations.meaning: Refuge, shelter, place of escape, safety vault, or fortress

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَوْ يَجِدُونَ مَلْجَأً أَوْ مَغَارَاتٍ أَوْ مُدَّخَلًا لَوَلَّوْا إِلَيْهِ وَهُمْ يَجْمَحُونَ (9:57): If they could find a refuge/shelter or caves or a hole to enter, they would turn to it and run wildly (hypocrites' battlefield panic).
discardedNoise: Political asylum claims in modern refugee treaties.
Taking shelter under trees during rain.
summaryEn: The root represents safety shelters (Malja'), detailing the absolute panic of hypocrites wishing for any cave/refuge, and the certainty that there is no refuge from God except in Him.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الملاذ الآمن ومأوى التحصن من الخطر (ملجأ)، واصفاً ذعر المنافقين وتمنيهم أي مغارة للهرب، وشهادة القلوب بألا ملجأ من الله إلا إليه.
rawText: إِلْتَجَاَ 8 see 1 and 4. الجأه إِلى شَىْءٍ 4 He constrained, compelled, forced, drove, or necessitated, him to have recourse to, or to betake himself to, or to repair to, or to do, a thing; he impelled him, or drove him, against his will, to it, or to do it; (S, * Mgh, Msb, K, * TA;) as also 🡻لجّاهُإِلَيْهِ. (Mgh, Msb.) === الجأ أَمْرَهُ إِلَى اللّٰهِ, (S, K,) and [إِلَى اللّٰهِ] 🡻لَجَأَ, and 🡻التجأ, and 🡻تلجّأ (TA,) He referred, or committed, his affair to God. (S, K.) === الجأه He protected him, defended him. (K.) Also said of a place, [It protected him; afforded him refuge.] (K, art. حرز.) === الجأه إِلَى شَىْءٍ is also said when one has defended another, [as] in a place of refuge; [and app. may be rendered He caused him to have recourse to a thing, as to a place of refuge]. (TA.) تَلَجَّاَ 5 see 1 and 4. لجّأ 2 , inf. n. تَلْجِئَةٌ, He forced a person to do a thing against his will: (S, K:) or, to do a thing which was contrary to what it appeared to be. (AHeyth.) === تَلْجِئَةٌ is also explained as signifying The leaving one's property to some one or more of his heirs, in preference to, or to the exclusion of, the other or others. لجّأ He so left his property. (ISh.) === See 4. لَجَأٌ and 🡻مَلْجَأٌ (S, K) and 🡻مُلْتَجَأٌ (K, art. لحد; &c.) A place to which one has recourse for refuge, protection, preservation, concealment, covert, or lodging; a place of refuge; an asylum; a refuge. (S, K.) The hemzeh of the ↓ second is sometimes elided; and this is done to assimilate the word to مَنْجَا, when it is used therewith; like as منجا is written with hemzeh to assimilate it in the like case to مَلْجَأٌ. === [ 🡻ملجأٌ is often applied to a man: and you say also,] 🡻المَلْجَإِفُلَانٌ حَسَنُ [Such a one is a good person to whom to have recourse for protection or concealment]. The pl. of لَجَأٌ is أَلْجَآءٌ. (TA.) === لَجَأٌ † A wife. (L.) === An heir. (ISh.) [See 2.] ___ The frog: (K:) or a kind of tortoise, that lives on the land and in the sea: (M:) fem. with ة. (K.) The لجأة of the sea (اللَّجَأَةُ البَحْرِيَّةُ) is asserted to have a tongue in its breast, and to kill the animal that it strikes. (Dmr.) لَجَأَ إِلَيْهِ 1 , aor. ﹷ, (S, K,) inf. n. لَجْءٌ (TA) or لَجَأٌ (S) and لُجُوْءٌ (TA) and مَلْجَأٌ; (S;) and لَجِئّ, aor. ﹷ, (K,) inf. n. لَجَأٌ; (TA;) and 🡻التجأ; (S, K;) He had recourse to it, or betook himself to it, or repaired to it, (i. e. a thing or a place, TA) for refuge, protection, preservation, concealment, covert, or lodging. (K.) See 4. === لَجَأَ إِلَيْهِ, and 🡻التجأ, and 🡻تلجّأ, He relied upon, and sought aid from, him. (TA.) === لَجَأَ عَنْهُ, and 🡻التجأ, and 🡻تلجّأ, and تلجأ مِنْهُ, He declined, or turned away, from him, إِلَى غَيْرِهِ to another, [and had recourse to the latter.] (TA.) مَلْجَأٌ and مُلْتَجَأٌ: see لَجَأٌ.
